Based on typical presentation and anatomical considerations, I believe the correct option is **Option B - Omental Cyst**. This aligns well given the described features—mobility, consistency suggestive of fluid-filled nature—and location relative to the umbilicus and hypogastrium area where omentum can extend freely without being adhered deep into organs like ovaries or bowel structures commonly seen in other conditions listed.
